title: San Francisco Botanical Garden | Hours, Tickets & Info | GGGP
content: Explore 55 beautiful acres of landscaped gardens and open spaces that showcase more than 8,000 different kinds of plants from around the world. Andean Cloud Forest Australia California Chile Mediterranean Mesoamerican Cloud Forest New Zealand South Africa Southeast Asia Cloud Forest Temperate Asia. Ancient Plants Moon Viewing Garden Children’s Garden Conifer Lawn Celebration Garden Fountain Plaza Garden of Fragrance Great Meadow Succulent Garden Zellerbach Garden. San Francisco’s cool foggy climate is reminiscent of these highland regions in the tropics, so it is no wonder that our Andean Cloud Forest Collection has flourished. One of our most striking features are the Andean wax palms (*Ceroxylon quindiuense*), the highest occurring and tallest species of palm in the world. There is no denying the beauty and diversity of California’s landscapes, and as a result, its flora is the most diverse in the United States at over 6000 species. title: A Day Out at the San Francisco Botanical Garden ⋆ Jupiter & Dann
content: # A Day Out at the San Francisco Botanical Garden. One of the places that Dann was most excited for was the San Francisco Botanical Gardens, which is a huge gardens and green space with plants from all over the world! There are maps there too, which you can grab for free, that showcase the area and the different gardens. We walked around for quite some time, exploring all of the different flowers and plants there was. I love the idea of having a moon viewing platform in a garden – and it’s a really unique feature that I had not seen before at the other gardens that we have visited in London! This area was completely different from other aspects of the gardens – which is something super unique about the San Francisco Botanical Gardens – each area felt like a different biome from the other. The San Francisco Botanical Gardens is a place you can easily spend a few hours at, walking around and enjoying the plants around you.
